seoTitle: Tibbuc vs Tichborne — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Tibbuc and Tichborne. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Tibbuc and Tichborne.
heading: Tibbuc vs Tichborne — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Tibbuc, New South Wales and Tichborne, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Tibbuc has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Tichborne (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Tibbuc or Tichborne safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Tibbuc has a flood risk score of 22/100, while Tichborne scores 44/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Tibbuc scores 25/100 for bushfire risk. Tichborne scores 33/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Tibbuc or Tichborne?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Tibbuc has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.88% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
